text:
SUMMARY

This is AI generated summarization, which may have errors. For context, always refer to the full article.

MANILA, Philippines – Four members of Team Philippines see action on Tuesday, July 30, as rowerJoanie Delgacoresumes her Paris Olympics campaign, while judokaKiyomi Watanabe, swimmerKayla Sanchez, and boxerNesthy Petecioopen their respective bids.

Here is Team Philippines’ schedule (Manila time):

Nesthy Petecio puts on a boxing clinic as she trounces India’s Jaismine Lamboria via unanimous decision to advance to the round of 16 of the women’s 57kg division.

The five judges score it 30-27, 30-27, 30-27, 29-28, 29-28 for Petecio, who won silver in the same class in the Tokyo Games.

Petecio faces France’s Amina Zidani next.

Read the full storyhere.

Kayla Sanchez advances to the semifinals of the women’s 100-meter freestyle after finishing at joint 10th out of the 29 participants in the heats.

Breaking her previous Philippine record of 54.25 seconds, Sanchez clocks 53.67 seconds as she makes the 16-swimmer semifinal cutoff.

Read the full storyhere.

Kiyomi Watanabe suffers another early Olympic exit as she falls prey to China’s Tang Jing in the round of 32 of the women’s -63kg class.

The contest lasts just 51 seconds as Tang, who is ranked No. 22 in the world, nails the win via ippon.

It is a heartbreaking Olympic return for Watanabe, who also got the boot in the round of 32 in the Tokyo Games three years ago.

Read the full storyhere.

Joanie Delgaco bows out of contention in the women’s single sculls as she finishes sixth and last in her quarterfinal race with a time of 7 minutes and 58.30 seconds.

Delgaco falls to the semifinals C/D.

Reigning Olympic champion Emma Twigg of New Zealand tops the race with 7:26.89 as she qualifies for the semifinals A/B alongside Switzerland’s Aurelia-Maxima Katharina Janzen (7:31.12) and Spain’s Virginia Diaz Rivas (7:34.01).

Only the top three in each of the four quarterfinal races advance to the semifinals A/B, with the bottom three relegated to the semifinals C/D.

Read the full storyhere.

Guaranteed a top-24 finish in the women’s single sculls, Delgaco aims to make it further by doing well in her quarterfinal race, where the three fastest will advance to the semifinals A/B.

Watanabe returns to the Olympics hoping to redeem herself following an early exit in the Tokyo Games as she faces China’s Tang Jing in the round of 32 of the women’s -63kg division.

Also back in the quadrennial meet after helping Canada win a pair of relay medals in Tokyo, Sanchez – now representing the Philippines – competes in the heats of the women’s 100m freestyle, needing to be among the 16 fastest to reach the semifinals.

Ending the day for Team Philippines is Petecio, who aims to complete her unfinished business after settling for silver in Tokyo as she tangles with India’s Jaismine Lamboria in the round of 32 of the women’s 57kg division.– Rappler.com

title: HIGHLIGHTS, RESULTS: Team Philippines, 2024 Paris Olympics – July 30
author: delfin.dioquino editor
description: Rower Joanie Delgaco resumes her Paris Olympics campaign, while judoka Kiyomi Watanabe, swimmer Kayla Sanchez, and boxer Nesthy Petecio open their respective bids
